title: Cirrus: Use images w/ buildah fix

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Some day, our CI will start working, and the PR queue will start to shrink. But it is clearly not this day.

@mheon by the looks of it, it's only papr causing trouble here.  I'm finishing up #1606 which can replace the papr-F28 job.  Following that is #1674 to replace FAH-28.  CentOS and RHEL images are ready for testing to be switched on.  The future looks bright, and my pipeline of work is totally full :smile: 

(This PR is ready to merge BTW)

The issue is the version of podman in F28 is broken. and this is causing all of the issues.  We need to get podman 0.10.1 into our test machines, and that should clear this up so that --privileged will start working again correctly.

@rhatdan this PR only affects cirrus, which currently does full-VM (make local...) testing on Ubuntu.  I haven't gotten to the podman-in-podman/docker-in-podman testing in cirrus yet, but it's in my pipeline.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1660) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
